data.content:
### List Sandbox Templates (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.5.2/template/page.mdx

Lists all available sandbox templates. It can filter templates by team ID.

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
 - `-t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize New Sandbox Template with SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.4/template/page.mdx

Initializes a new sandbox template using the E2B SDK. Allows specifying the path, template name, and target language (typescript, python-sync, python-async).

```bash
e2b template init-v2 [options]
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Define MIME Type Alias

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.2.0/messaging/page.mdx

The `MIMEType` type alias is a simple string alias for representing MIME types, commonly used to specify the nature and format of a document or data.

```typescript
type MIMEType: string;
```

--------------------------------

### List Sandbox Templates (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.6.0/template/page.mdx

Lists all available sandbox templates associated with a team. Requires specifying the team ID.

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
 - -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
```

--------------------------------

### Unpublish Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.8.0/template/page.mdx

Unpublishes a sandbox template, making it no longer publicly available. Options include specifying the path, config file, team ID, and interactively selecting a template or skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template unpublish [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
  -y, --yes: skip manual unpublish confirmation
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.7.0/template/page.mdx

Removes a sandbox template and its associated e2b.toml configuration file. Supports specifying the path, configuration file, team ID, and options to select templates interactively or skip confirmation.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]

Options:
 - -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list 
 - -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - -y, --yes: skip manual delete confirmation
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox from Specified Template - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.4.0/sandbox/page.mdx

This overload of the static `create` method allows for the instantiation of a new sandbox from a specific sandbox template, identified by its name or ID. It accepts the template identifier and optional connection options (`SandboxOpts`), returning a Promise that resolves to the created sandbox instance. An example illustrates how to use it with a template name.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, template: string, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>

/**
 * Create a new sandbox from the specified sandbox template.
 *
 * @template S The type of the Sandbox class.
 * @param this The Sandbox class.
 * @param template The sandbox template name or ID.
 * @param opts Optional connection options.
 * @returns A Promise resolving to the sandbox instance for the new sandbox.
 */

// Example:
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')
```

--------------------------------

### List Available Sandbox Templates with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.7.0/template/page.mdx

Lists all available sandbox templates associated with the specified team. Requires a team ID for proper filtering.

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
 - -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
```

--------------------------------

### List Sandbox Templates with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.9.1/template/page.mdx

Lists all available sandbox templates associated with a specific team. Requires the team ID as an option.

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
 - `-t, --team <team-id>`: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
```

--------------------------------

### List Sandbox Templates

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.2/template/page.mdx

Lists available sandbox templates. Supports filtering by team ID and specifying the output format, such as JSON or pretty print.

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
  -f, --format <format>: output format, eg. json, pretty
```

--------------------------------

### Build Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.5.1/template/page.mdx

Builds a sandbox template from a Dockerfile. It supports specifying the Dockerfile path, template name, start command, and various build-time configurations. This command creates an e2b.toml configuration file alongside the template.

```bash
e2b template build [options] [template]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>`: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - `-d, --dockerfile <file>`: specify path to Dockerfile. By default E2B tries to find e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in root directory. 
 - `-n, --name <template-name>`: specify sandbox template name. You can use the template name to start the sandbox with SDK. The template name must be lowercase and contain only letters, numbers, dashes and underscores. 
 - `-c, --cmd <start-command>`: specify command that will be executed when the sandbox is started. 
 - `--ready-cmd <ready-command>`: specify command that will need to exit 0 for the template to be ready. 
 - `-t, --team <team-id>`: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - `--config <e2b-toml>`: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - `--cpu-count <cpu-count>`: specify the number of CPUs that will be used to run the sandbox. The default value is 2. 
 - `--memory-mb <memory-mb>`: specify the amount of memory in megabytes that will be used to run the sandbox. Must be an even number. The default value is 512. 
 - `--build-arg <args...>`: specify additional build arguments for the build command. The format should be <varname>=<value>. 
 - `--no-cache`: skip cache when building the template. 
```

--------------------------------

### Type Aliases

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.1.0/messaging/page.mdx

Definitions for custom types used within the E2B API.

```APIDOC
## Type Aliases

### Logs

```ts
type Logs: object;
```

Data printed to stdout and stderr during execution, usually by print statements, logs, warnings, subprocesses, etc.

#### Type declaration

| Name | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `stderr` | `string`[] | List of strings printed to stderr by prints, subprocesses, etc. |
| `stdout` | `string`[] | List of strings printed to stdout by prints, subprocesses, etc. |

***

### MIMEType

```ts
type MIMEType: string;
```

Represents a MIME type.

***

### RawData

```ts
type RawData: object & E2BData;
```

Dictionary that maps MIME types to their corresponding representations of the data.

```
